name: Mairee
desc: Earth-like planet orbiting Yra, a G-type star. Has a breathable atmosphere, liquid water, three moons (Skuld, Verdandi, Urdra), and is home to the planet-wide Myco-Rhizomic System.
narration: Our second home. The promise. The lie. From orbit, she looks like a jewel. We are the infection. She knows it.

name: The Sickle
desc: A geologically stable basin shielded by a semi-circular mountain range. Site of the main colony. Has phosphorescent green mists at the mountain summits.
narration: A basin between mountains, shielded from the worst of the weather. We built a town here. The mountains glow at the summits — green, phosphorescent — and nobody has bothered to ask why.

name: The Plateau
desc: A geologically ancient basaltic shield. Cold (-60°C to -18°C), thin-aired, radioactive. Site of the Ivaldi industrial base, the Maw ore processor, and the Masonic chambers.
narration: The opposite of paradise. Cold. Thin air. Radiation. We dropped the Ivaldi onto it and called it home. The Masons built a temple in the basement. Of course they did.

name: The Delta
desc: A rich, highly dangerous jungle biome. Lethally integrated ecosystem: shimmercat predators, burr-vines, dartswarms, clackers, gramophone plants, vurnshafts, and the active MRS.
narration: A churning petri dish. The perfect place to plant a garden — if you like crops that bite back. Everything here is alive. Most of it is opinionated.

name: The Leaf
desc: Survey Camp “The Leaf” (Site Prospect Alpha-7, Mairee Delta) is the colony’s primary high-risk outpost for foraging and water extraction, overseeing a distributed network of DROP sites that supply roughly 20% of total potable water. Embedded within the volatile Myco-Rhizomic System (MRS), the camp sits in an environment likened to a “churning petri dish,” marked by extreme humidity, sweet-rotting compost odors, and recurring psychedelic spore storms, where terrain can unpredictably liquefy or calcify. Physically, it forms a defensive “scar in the forest,” composed of hexagonal modular habitats clustered around a central command tent and enclosed by a fortified hexagonal perimeter of barricades, automated turrets, and sonic deterrents. The site operates under constant biological threat—from predatory fauna such as Shimmercats, Clackers, and Dartswarms to invasive flora including microscopic Burr-vines and reactive Gramophone Plants—resulting in a persistently unstable operational state where overstretched personnel maintain only a fragile equilibrium against an ecosystem actively attempting to reclaim the installation.
narration: The colony's foothold in the Delta. A scar in the forest, ringed with turrets. The forest is patient. The turrets are not.

name: The Caldera
desc: A crown-shaped volcanic valley discovered via vurnshaft tunnels. Rich soil, sheltered by high walls. Marco Thieman establishes a site here.
narration: A volcanic valley shaped like a crown, hidden inside the jungle. Thieman found it. He brought his people there. They will run out of reasons to come back.

name: Orbital Three
desc: Small orbital relay and comms station where Lieutenant Muro monitors the void. Now silent after the Fold's collapse. Muro's isolation here drives him to the edge of madness.
narration: Muro's man cave. Mostly listens to static now. The static, lately, listens back.

name: Orbital Command
desc: The orbital command station formed from the Trunk section of the FNS Yggdrasil. Controls the Corridor guidance system, Vanguard patrol craft, and all orbital logistics.
narration: The trunk of the old Yggdrasil. The brain that decides what moves where through the Corridor. Bellamy lives here. So does her wine.

name: FreyOps Central
desc: The Directorate's surface command center at the rim of the Homestead residential district. Location of the Grid control room and main holoscreens monitoring the colony.
narration: The Directorate's surface office. Holoscreens. Guardians. A polished floor that has never seen mud. From here, the Grid is watching.

name: The Star Chamber
desc: A deep cavern in the Plateau containing a wall of obsidian-like crystal with thousands of moving lights inside — forming constellations and geometric shapes. Reactive to vibration.
narration: A cavern at the bottom of the Plateau where the wall is alive. Lights move inside the crystal. The drills make them dance faster. When we stop, they slow. The planet has noticed us.

name: The Anchor
desc: A secret chamber inside the Ivaldi base with a checkerboard floor, star-chart dome, six white banners, a Trestle Board of dark glass, and a stone sarcophagus.
narration: A perfect room hidden in the rock. Checkerboard floor. Star-chart ceiling. Six banners. A sarcophagus. The Masons did not build this. Or — if they did — they were doing the bidding of someone older.

name: The Zaytsev Aperture (Fold)
aliases: The Fold, the Aperture, Zaytsev Fold
desc: A spatial fold discovered near Sol connecting to the Yreus system. Collapsed after the Brisinger's transit. Its loss strands the colony permanently.
narration: The door we came through. Closed behind us. We watched it close. Nobody has yet said the word permanently. Permanently.

name: Freya Harbor / Port
desc: The main docking and landing facility at the Homestead. Contains docking cranes, cargo drones on magnetic rails, and the landing grid. Site of the Brisinger's crash.
narration: Where we dock the things we still have. Cranes. Cargo drones. The Brisinger landed here. Landed is generous.

name: The Corridor
desc: The orbital guidance network enabling smooth transit between the Homestead, Delta, Plateau, and orbital stations. Ground-based relay antennas dot the landscape.
narration: Bellamy's tightrope. A guidance lattice that lets ships move between settlements without hitting the weather. Also tells the Directorate where you are. Both, at all times.

name: Muspel
desc: The closest planet to Yra. A scorched, iron-rich world. Temperatures high enough to melt lead on its dayside. No atmosphere worth speaking of — what little gas clings to its surface is metallic vapour. Geologically violent and entirely inhospitable. Nobody has proposed a use for it yet.
narration: The first planet. A scorched iron ball close enough to its sun to melt the lead in a bullet. Nobody has proposed a use for it yet. Nobody likely will.

name: Ysk
desc: Third planet of the Yreus system. A cold desert with a thin carbon dioxide atmosphere and polar ice caps of frozen water. Surface temperatures average -60°C. Possible future outpost — the ice caps suggest subsurface liquid water may have existed, and may exist still. It would be a difficult one. Everything here would be.
narration: The third planet. A cold desert with polar ice caps. Liquid water may have existed once. Some part of us thinks — we could live here, if Mairee turns on us. Some part of us already does.

name: The Ginnunga Gap
desc: A wide, dense asteroid belt beyond Ysk — far larger and more concentrated than Sol's own. Named for the mythological void of Norse cosmology. Rich in silicates, iron-nickel, and volatile ices. Navigation through it is technically possible but practically dangerous without good sensor coverage. The Muninn and Hugin would have been useful here.
narration: An asteroid belt larger than Sol's. Wide. Dense. Full of useful metals and one immortal navigational headache. The science ships we lost would have been useful here. They are not here.

name: Ymir
desc: The larger of Yreus's two gas giants. Dark and majestic, with deep charcoal-grey banding and a system of rings — thin and dark, absorbing rather than reflecting light. Ymir has the silent authority of something enormous and indifferent. Its gravity well is a navigational hazard for anything on a low-fuel trajectory inbound from the Fold.
narration: The big gas giant. Dark banded. Quiet. Indifferent. The kind of presence that does not notice you, and that — somehow — you notice anyway.

name: Nefir
desc: The outermost planet of the Yreus system. A smaller ice giant that glows deep blue in the distant twilight — methane-rich upper atmosphere scattering short wavelengths back into the void. Cold enough to freeze nitrogen. Barely visible from Mairee with the naked eye. It looks like a star that never quite made it.
narration: The outermost. A small blue ice giant on the far edge of the system. Visible from Mairee, barely. Looks like a star that didn't make it.

name: Skuld
desc: The largest of Mairee's three moons. Named for the Norn of the future. Tidally locked, heavily cratered on its far side. Its gravitational interaction with Mairee is thought to contribute to the planet's unusually stable axial tilt — one of the reasons Mairee was hospitable enough to colonise in the first place.
narration: Mairee's biggest moon. Named for the Norn of the future. Holds the planet steady. One of the reasons we are alive here. Allegedly.

name: Verdandi
desc: The middle moon of Mairee. Named for the Norn of the present. Smaller and faster than Skuld, completing its orbit in just over a Mairee day. From the surface, it rises and sets twice in a single click. Some colonists track it compulsively — there is something about its speed that feels like time passing faster than it should.
narration: The middle moon. Named for the Norn of the present. Fast — rises and sets twice in a click. Some colonists track her. Something about her speed feels like time passing faster than it should.

name: Urdra
desc: The smallest and most distant of Mairee's three moons. Named for the Norn of the past. Barely more than a captured asteroid — irregular, dark, with a retrograde orbit suggesting it arrived from the Ginnunga Gap. It rarely appears in the same part of the sky as Skuld or Verdandi. It keeps its own schedule.
narration: The smallest moon. Named for the Norn of the past. Probably a captured asteroid. Keeps her own schedule. Does not align with the others. Rarely seen in the same part of the sky.

name: Kepler Array
desc: A deep-space communications relay positioned near the Sol mouth of the Zaytsev Fold to maximize signal range across the transit gap. Designed to bridge transmissions between the Sol system and the Yreus colony fleet. Became permanently silent when the Fold collapsed. Currently drifting — no crew, no function, no future.
narration: The phone line home. Sat on the Sol side of the Fold, waiting to bridge transmissions across the gap. The gap is now permanent. Kepler is now drifting. Nothing about her works anymore.

name: Orbit One
desc: The surveillance and weather monitoring station in close orbit around Mairee. Originally part of the FNS Yggdrasil network. Tracks atmospheric events, spore storm formation, and surface movement. Data feeds directly to Orbital Command and FreyOps Central. Also known, less charitably, as the Peeping Tom. Nobody asks who else it watches.
narration: Watches the weather. Tells us when the spore storms are coming. Also watches us. Less charitable name: the Peeping Tom. Nobody asks who else she watches.

name: Orbital Two
desc: Atmospheric processing station in close orbit. Strips usable particles from the upper atmosphere — oxygen, nitrogen, trace compounds — and compresses them for life support distribution across the orbital network and surface facilities. Without it, the stations stop breathing. It does not stop working.
narration: Strips usable particles from the upper atmosphere and pipes them to life support. The stations breathe because she breathes. She does not stop.

name: Solbay
desc: The colonial departure hub — docking, provisioning, final departure. The Freya fleet launched from here. Most of the people aboard had never gone past Solbay before.
narration: The harbor. Where they taught you to dream of the stars before kicking you into the void.

name: Sol Academy
desc: The training academy in orbit around Earth. Candidates trained, tested, and were told they were ready.
narration: The academy. Where they trained you, tested you, and told you you were ready. None of you were ready. None of you are.

name: Yreus Relay
desc: A covert, asymmetrical station at the Yreus Relay, commandeered by General Dennis Reed.
narration: A covert station at the Yreus relay. Asymmetrical. Sketchy. Commandeered by General Reed for purposes he has not yet explained. He will. Probably.
